rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MILITINI MAUGATA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The published notice referred to the "AMENDED" petition, even though the subject petition has never been amended. The Court is to determine whether the defect should be waived. 2) The public administrator has not waived priority for appointment as administrator, in the absence of a guardian of the estate of the minor beneficiary who could have asserted priority on the minor's behalf by nominating the petitioner or other person as administrator under Probate Code section 8465(a)(3). It should be noted that the decedent's parents and siblings who nominated the petitioner have no priority under section 8465(a)(2), as they are not relatives entitled to succeed to any part of the estate within the meaning of section 8462. 3) The Court is to determine whether the petitioner's bond should be waived. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE RODRIGUEZ 2008 TRUST-EXEMPTION TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Felonious And Intentional Killing Of Settlor And Ascertain Beneficiaries Of Exemption Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Probate Code section 250(a)(1) provides that a person who feloniously and intentionally kills the decedent is not entitled to any property, interest, or benefit under a trust created by the decedent. Section 250(b)(1) provides that the property, interest, or benefit referred to in section 250(a)(1) passes as if the killer had predeceased the decedent. The petitioners are remainder beneficiaries of Exemption Trust entitled to receive distribution on the surviving spouse/settlor's death if the predeceased spouse/settlor is Mary Rodriguez. The petitioners are also remainder beneficiaries of Survivor's Trust entitled to receive distribution on the surviving spouse/settlor's death if the surviving spouse/settlor is Mary Rodriguez. The petition requests the Court's determination that the petitioners' remainder interests in Exemption Trust have been accelerated by the murder conviction of Roberto Rodriguez whose interest in Exemption Trust as the sole income beneficiary during his lifetime has been skipped by operation of section 250(b)(1). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. No conformed copy of signed order will be returned (no self-addressed stamped envelope provided). Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 08/01/2023. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 08/08/2023.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SALIM UDDIN KHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/16/2022 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 07/11/2022). Notes were e-mailed to counsel on 07/12/2022, even though no courtesy copy of the petition and proposed order had been received.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NATHANIEL JAMES JOHNSTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/10/2022 to allow e-filings to be processed and to be heard with related matter. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATALINA CAMACLANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/9/22. Examiner's Notes emailed on 7/11/22. Note: tentative ruling continuances are final and uncontestable and are not subject to Local Rule 14.10.B.
